---
title: "Prepare network files for analysis in Cytoscape, control"
format: html
date: "2026-05-11"
bibliography: ../../../refs-stat-anal.bib
execute:
  fig-format: png
  fig.dpi: 300
---

The aim of this code is to prepare files (clustering results and String network data [@szklarczykSTRINGDatabase20232023]) for Cytoscape analysis and visualization [@clineIntegrationBiologicalNetworks2007]. Separately for each cluster.
Using 0.7 membership cutoff.
This script uses GO-annotation and associated files from UniProt, prepared in [00-test-filter.qmd](../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/00-test-filter.qmd) and [00-GO-and-mapping-UniProt.qmd](../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/00-GO-and-mapping-UniProt.qmd).
Here option 1) using NAM - GO is realized.
From clusters genes with specific, selected annotations are selected.

:::{.callout-note}
This script is a greatly improved version of sum of the [10-NAMzUP-leaf-networks-cytoscape-loop-ctrl.qmd](../../../vs-day-one/leaf/scripts/10-NAMzUP-leaf-networks-cytoscape-loop-ctrl.qmd) and [10-NAMzUP-leaf-rel3-networks-cytoscape-ctrl.qmd](../../../vs-day-one/leaf/scripts/10-NAMzUP-leaf-rel3-networks-cytoscape-ctrl.qmd). Joining part was changed from `bash` to `R`.
:::

Built with R version `r getRversion()`

## R Preliminaries

Environment cleaning and set-up

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
#| output: false

# Clean working directory
rm(list=ls())
```

## Load data

`cl14_m1.25` contains data for clusters.


```{r load}
load("../rdata-saved/mfuzz_cl14_m1.25_leaf.RDa")

# gene description
anno = read.table("../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/NAM_descr_best", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, sep="\t", quote = "")
colnames(anno) <- c("gene", "description")

```

## Prepare and export cluster data

```{bash net-dir}
rm -r ../results/network/
mkdir ../results/network/
```

```{r membership-table}
## All genes in clusters with membership values
## 1.  Sprawdź, co w nim jest
# str(cl14_m1.25)

## 2.  Pobierz wektor cluster (nazwany) i macierz membership
cl_vec   <- cl14_m1.25$cluster               # wektor długości 11936, nazwy = geny
mem_mat  <- cl14_m1.25$membership            # macierz 11936 × 10

## 3.  Upewnij się, że nazwy wierszy obu obiektów są zgodne
identical(names(cl_vec), rownames(mem_mat))

## 4.  Stwórz data‑frame z dwoma zestawami kolumn
df <- data.frame(
  cluster = cl_vec,                     # pierwsza kolumna – numer klas
  mem_mat,                              # kolejne 10 kolumn – membership
  check.names = FALSE                   # pozwala na nazwę "1", "2", …
)

## 5.  (Opcjonalnie) nadaj sensowne nazwy kolumnom membership
colnames(df)[2:ncol(df)] <- paste0("mem_", seq_len(ncol(mem_mat)))

# make column from rownames

df2 <- df |>
  mutate(gene = rownames(df)) |>   # nowa kolumna "gene"
  select(gene, everything())             # opcjonalnie przestaw kolejność

# select only rows where any of the memberships is >= 0.7
cl14_m1.25_mem07 <- df2 |>
  filter(
    if_any(starts_with("mem_"),
           ~ . >= 0.7)              # warunek na każdą z nich
  )


# Extract max value from membership columns and retain it in new column
# 1 Wybieramy kolumny zaczynające się od "mem_"
mem_cols <- grep("^mem_", names(cl14_m1.25_mem07), value = TRUE)

# 2 Obliczamy maksimum w każdym wierszu (ignorujemy NA)
max_mem <- apply(cl14_m1.25_mem07[mem_cols], 1, max, na.rm = TRUE)

# 3 Tworzymy nową ramkę z wybranymi kolumnami + maksymalną wartością
cl14_m1.25_maxmem <- data.frame(
  gene        = cl14_m1.25_mem07$gene,
  cluster     = cl14_m1.25_mem07$cluster,
  max_mem     = max_mem,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Table will be joined with String network data by gene name so it must be sorted

cl14_m1.25_sort <- cl14_m1.25_maxmem[order(cl14_m1.25_maxmem$gene), ]

write.table(cl14_m1.25_sort, file="../results/network/cl14_m1.25_sort", sep="\t", quote=FALSE, row.names=FALSE)

```

## Add UniProt-NAM mapping, prepared in `00-GO-and-mapping-UniProt.qmd`

Preferentially UPs in STRING were selected already.


```{bash clean-network}
##### This step must be done only once! #####
#
# removing unnecessary prefix from IDs in whole network
## This step uses source file stored outside project directory tree and temporarily use home dir
#zcat /media/mj/ANTIX-LIVE/siec-ala-string/4577.protein.links.full.v12.0.txt.gz | sed 's/4577\.//g' > ~/full-network
#pigz --best ~/full-network
#mv ~/full-network.gz ../../../input-data/networks-up/
#
#############################################
```

```{bash UPs-NAM}
##### This step must be done only once! #####
# 
# cd ../../../input-data/networks-up/
# 
# # change order of columns
# awk -F'\t' -v OFS='\t' '{print $2,$1}' ../go-uniprot/NAM2UP_all_forGOextraction > best-nam-string-ids
# 
# # prepare dictionary for sed
# awk -F'\t' '{print "s/"$2"/"$1"/g"}' best-nam-string-ids > nam-up-dict
#############################################
```

## Join cluster data with UP-NAM mapping

```{bash join-cluster-nam}
##### This step must be done only once for a given 'c' and 'm' combination! #####

cd ../results/network/

# join and sort by UP
join -1 2 -2 1 -t $'\t' ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/networks-up/best-nam-string-ids cl14_m1.25_sort | sort -k2,2 -t $'\t'> c14_UP_srt

# checks - NAMs are unique but UP no
wc -l c14_UP_srt
# 12671 c14_UP_srt
cut -f1 -d $'\t' c14_UP_srt | sort -u | wc -l
# 12671
cut -f2 -d $'\t' c14_UP_srt | sort -u | wc -l
# 12620 # some UPs map to multiple NAMs

# Check
cut -f2 -d $'\t' c14_UP_srt | sort | uniq -d > x
grep -Fwf x c14_UP_srt > x2
cut -f2,3 -d $'\t' x2 | sort -u > x3 # unique sort keeps only one row for genes duplicated in the same cluster
cut -f1 x3 | uniq -c | tr -s ' ' | grep -v ' 1' | cut -f3 -d ' ' > xduplicated # keep proteins present in > 1 row

# look/count at the duplicated proteins
grep -f xduplicated x2 | wc -l
# 46 NAMs
wc -l xduplicated
# 26 proteins corresponding to duplicated NAMs are in different clusters

# exclude them
grep -Fwvf xduplicated c14_UP_srt > x2
mv x2 c14_UP_srt
rm x*
```

## Add network data

```{bash prep for join}
cd ../results/network/
  
## extract data for first interactors from network file
  
# cut UPs column, this was done previously BUT this time it won't contain duplicates in different clusters
cut -f2 -d $'\t' c14_UP_srt | sort -u > c14_ids_up

# extract interactions for selected UPs from full network
zgrep -Fwf c14_ids_up ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/networks-up/full-network.gz > c14_UP_net_selected
```

```{r join-net-cluster}
## Add NAMs and description ##

# translate spaces to tabs in network data

xUP1 = read.table("../results/network/c14_UP_net_selected", sep=' ', header = FALSE)
colnames(xUP1) = c("uniprot1", "uniprot2", "neighborhood", "neighborhood_transferred", "fusion", "cooccurence", "homology", "coexpression", "coexpression_transferred", "experiments", "experiments_transferred", "database", "database_transferred", "textmining", "textmining_transferred", "combined_score")

NAM_UP_anno = read.table("../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/NAM-UP-anno", sep='\t', header = FALSE, quote = "")
colnames(NAM_UP_anno) = c('ensembl', 'uniprot', 'desc')

# UP1

x2 = left_join(xUP1, NAM_UP_anno, 
               by = c("uniprot1" = "uniprot"),
               relationship = "many-to-many") |>
      relocate(ensembl1 = ensembl, desc1 = desc, .after = uniprot1)

# UP2

xUPsNAMs = left_join(x2, NAM_UP_anno,
                     by = c('uniprot2' = 'uniprot'),
                     relationship = "many-to-many") |>
      relocate(ensembl2 = ensembl, desc2 = desc, .after = uniprot2)


## Add cluster data ##

# NAM1

x2NAM1 = left_join(xUPsNAMs, cl14_m1.25_sort,
                   by = c('ensembl1' = 'gene'),
                   relationship = "many-to-one") |>
      relocate(cluster1 = cluster, member1 = max_mem, .after = desc1)

# NAM2

x3NAM = left_join(x2NAM1, cl14_m1.25_sort,
                       by = c('ensembl2' = 'gene'),
                       relationship = "many-to-one") |>
      relocate(cluster2 = cluster, member2 = max_mem, .after = desc2) |>
        filter(!is.na(cluster1))

# Save to file
write.table(x3NAM, file="~/x4final", sep = '\t', na = "", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)

rm(list=ls())
```

```{bash checks}
cd ../results/network/
## Checks
# NAM - UP mapping
# cut NAMs and UPs for both interacting proteins
cut -f1,2 -d $'\t' ~/x4final > ~/x1
cut -f6,7 -d $'\t' ~/x4final > ~/x2
# concatenate
cat ~/x1 ~/x2 | sort -u > ~/x3
# translate NAMs to UPs
sed -f ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/networks-up/nam-up-dict ~/x3 > ~/x
# select rows where UP2 are missing
awk -v FS='\t' -v OFS='\t' '$1!=$2 {print $2}' ~/x | sort -u
# should be empty oh have only col. names

# NAM - description
# cut corresponding columns
cut -f2,3 -d $'\t' ~/x4final | sort -u > ~/x1
cut -f7,8 -d $'\t' ~/x4final | sort -u > ~/x2
# concatenate
cat ~/x1 ~/x2 | sort -u > ~/x3
# compare to file with description
join -j1 -t $'\t' ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/NAM_descr_best ~/x3 > ~/x4
# select rows with different data in description
awk -v FS='\t' -v OFS='\t' '$2!=$3' ~/x4 | sort -u
# should be empty

## Check NAM - cluster assignment
# extract respecive columns
cut -f2,4,5 -d $'\t' ~/x4final | sort -u > ~/x1
cut -f7,9,10 -d $'\t' ~/x4final | sort -u > ~/x2
# concatenate
cat ~/x1 ~/x2 | sort -u > ~/x3
# does data present in both files match?
join -j1 -t $'\t' cl14_m1.25_sort ~/x3 > ~/x4
# cut respective columns and compare with diff
cut -f2,3 -d $'\t' ~/x4 > ~/x41
cut -f4,5 -d $'\t' ~/x4 > ~/x42
diff -s ~/x41 ~/x42
# Pliki /home/mj/x41 i /home/mj/x42 są identyczne

# Check NAMs not in cluster
# extract NAMs without cluster from final file
join -j1 -t $'\t' -a1 -a2 -o auto cl14_m1.25_sort ~/x3 | awk -v FS='\t' -v OFS='\t' '$2=="" && $4==""' > ~/x5
# grep them from network assignment file
grep -Fwf ~/x5 cl14_m1.25_sort > ~/x2
# check number of rows, should be 0
wc -l ~/x2
# 0 /home/mj/x2

## Check UP - UP assignment
# extract from final file
cut -f1,6 -d $'\t' ~/x4final | tr -d '\t' | sort -u > ~/x
# extract info from full network
zcat ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/networks-up/full-network.gz | cut -f1,2 -d' ' | tr -d ' ' | tail -n +2 | sort -u > ~/x2
# grep data from final file from full network - all should be there
grep -Fwf ~/x ~/x2 > ~/x3
# compare
diff -s  ~/x3 ~/x
# Pliki /home/mj/x3 i /home/mj/x są identyczne

# Cleanup

mv ~/x4final c14_net_allcl
rm ~/x*

# Manual checks for random entries
shuf -n3 c14_net_allcl

### Examples
## input-data/go-uniprot dir
# NAM - UP assignment
grep Zm00001eb314790 ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/BEST_UPs
grep A0A1D6LKH6 ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/BEST_UPs

# NAM and UP description
grep Zm00001eb269840 ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/NAM_descr_best
grep A0A1D6LKH6 ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/UP_descr_best

## Cluster assignment
# leaf/results/network dir
grep Zm00001eb269840 cl14_m1.25_sort

```

## Split by cluster

```{bash split}
cd ../results/network/

# using 400 score cutoff
awk -v FS='\t' -v OFS='\t' '$24>=400 { print > "cluster_"$4"_400.txt" }' c14_net_allcl

# add header

echo -e "uniprot1\tensembl1\tdesc1\tcluster1\tmember1\tuniprot2\tensembl2\tdesc2\tcluster2\tmember2\tneighborhood\tneighborhood_transferred\tfusion\tcooccurence\thomology\tcoexpression\tcoexpression_transferred\texperiments\texperiments_transferred\tdatabase\tdatabase_transferred\ttextmining\ttextmining_transferred\tcombined_score" > header-net-tab

for i in {1..14}; do
  cat header-net-tab cluster_${i}_400.txt > temp && mv temp cluster_${i}_400.txt ;
done

pigz --best c14_net_allcl
```

```{bash tf-activ}

cd ../results/network/

for i in {1..14}; do
awk '
    NR==FNR {           # Czytamy pierwszy plik (rel3-genes-tf_activ)
        keep[$1] = 1;       # budujemy tablicę asocjacyjną – klucz = wartość
       next;             # przejdź do kolejnego rekordu (nie analizujemy dalej)
    }
    $2 in keep {         # Drugi plik (klastry); sprawdzamy kolumnę 2
        print               # wypisujemy cały wiersz, jeśli wartość jest w tablicy
    }
' ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/rel3-genes-tf_activ cluster_${i}_400.txt > x
  cat header-net-tab x > cluster_${i}_400_rel3_tf-activ.txt;
done

## Remove files without data

files=($(ls cluster_*_400_rel3_tf-activ.txt))

for file in ${files[@]}; do
# Przykład: liczymy linie w pliku data.txt
linijek=$(wc -l < ${file})   # < – przekierowanie, dzięki czemu wc zwraca samą liczbę

if (( linijek < 2 )); then
rm ${file}
fi
done
```

```{bash photosyn}
cd ../results/network/

for i in {1..14}; do
awk '
    NR==FNR {           # Czytamy pierwszy plik (rel3-genes-photosyn)
        keep[$1] = 1;       # budujemy tablicę asocjacyjną – klucz = wartość
       next;             # przejdź do kolejnego rekordu (nie analizujemy dalej)
    }
    $2 in keep {         # Drugi plik (klastry); sprawdzamy kolumnę 2
        print               # wypisujemy cały wiersz, jeśli wartość jest w tablicy
    }
' ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/rel3-genes-photosyn cluster_${i}_400.txt > x
  cat header-net-tab x > cluster_${i}_400_rel3_photosyn.txt;
done

## Remove files without data

files=($(ls cluster_*_400_rel3_photosyn.txt))

for file in ${files[@]}; do
# Przykład: liczymy linie w pliku data.txt
linijek=$(wc -l < ${file})   # < – przekierowanie, dzięki czemu wc zwraca samą liczbę

if (( linijek < 2 )); then
rm ${file}
fi
done
```

```{bash rhythm}
cd ../results/network/

for i in {1..14}; do
awk '
    NR==FNR {           # Czytamy pierwszy plik (rel3-genes-rhythm)
        keep[$1] = 1;       # budujemy tablicę asocjacyjną – klucz = wartość
       next;             # przejdź do kolejnego rekordu (nie analizujemy dalej)
    }
    $2 in keep {         # Drugi plik (klastry); sprawdzamy kolumnę 2
        print               # wypisujemy cały wiersz, jeśli wartość jest w tablicy
    }
' ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/rel3-genes-rhythm cluster_${i}_400.txt > x
  cat header-net-tab x > cluster_${i}_400_rel3_rhythm.txt;
done

## Remove files without data

files=($(ls cluster_*_400_rel3_rhythm.txt))

for file in ${files[@]}; do
# Przykład: liczymy linie w pliku data.txt
linijek=$(wc -l < ${file})   # < – przekierowanie, dzięki czemu wc zwraca samą liczbę

if (( linijek < 2 )); then
rm ${file}
fi
done
```

```{bash dev-rel}
cd ../results/network/

for i in {1..14}; do
awk '
    NR==FNR {           # Czytamy pierwszy plik (rel3-genes-dev)
        keep[$1] = 1;       # budujemy tablicę asocjacyjną – klucz = wartość
       next;             # przejdź do kolejnego rekordu (nie analizujemy dalej)
    }
    $2 in keep {         # Drugi plik (klastry); sprawdzamy kolumnę 2
        print               # wypisujemy cały wiersz, jeśli wartość jest w tablicy
    }
' ../../../../../project1-stat/input-data/go-uniprot/rel3-genes-dev cluster_${i}_400.txt > x
  cat header-net-tab x > cluster_${i}_400_rel3_dev.txt;
done

## Remove files without data

files=($(ls cluster_*_400_rel3_dev.txt))

for file in ${files[@]}; do
# Przykład: liczymy linie w pliku data.txt
linijek=$(wc -l < ${file})   # < – przekierowanie, dzięki czemu wc zwraca samą liczbę

if (( linijek < 2 )); then
rm ${file}
fi
done
```

## References
